title: epitemplate
text: epitemplate pkgdown template package provides consistent styling layout R package documentation sites maintained Economic Policy Institute (EPI). EPI’s R packages hosted : https://economic.r-universe.dev/builds Packages use template include: epiextractr, epidatatools, epitargets, realtalk, swadlr, prefab.

title: epitemplate
text: pkgdown template package supplies shared configuration, templates, styles, assets packages reference _pkgdown.yml: Consumer packages also add Config/Needs/website: economic/epitemplate DESCRIPTION GitHub Actions can find install template.

title: epitemplate
text: inst/pkgdown/_pkgdown.yml — shared bslib config: colors, fonts, navbar, footer structure inst/pkgdown/extra.scss — custom SCSS (font loading, heading weights, spacing, navbar, sidebar, footer, code blocks, links). Note: extra.css supported template packages. inst/pkgdown/templates/layout.html — custom layout places <footer> outside .container full-width border-top inst/pkgdown/assets/fonts/ — bundled Figtree Fira Code woff2 variable fonts R/test.R — exported test() function; exists roxygen docs (headings, code blocks) visually test reference page vignettes/template-preview.Rmd — vignette exercising text, bullets, numbered lists, code blocks, inline code, links

title: epitemplate
text: Edit SCSS/config inst/pkgdown/ Rebuild site: Rscript -e "pkgdown::build_site()" Hard-refresh browser (Ctrl+Shift+R) — CSS aggressively cached Check page types: home, reference index, reference detail, articles, changelog

title: epitemplate
text: Font paths extra.scss use ../../fonts/ compiled CSS lives docs/deps/ fonts docs/fonts/ SCSS changes require full site rebuild; browser cache must cleared custom layout.html must stay sync pkgdown’s default layout — footer placement differs font_scale bslib config affects spacing globally, just font size

title: Template preview
text: Economic Policy Institute’s CPS extracts contain microdata Current Population Survey, including variables earnings, hours, demographics, union status. extracts maintained EPI distributed epiextractr R package. Researchers can load ORG, Basic Monthly, March, May supplement files. columns selected default, users can specify subset variables faster loading lower memory use.

title: Template preview
text: Key variables CPS ORG extracts: year month — survey reference period wage — hourly wage (set hourly earnings hourly workers, usual weekly earnings divided usual hours non-hourly workers) orgwgt — CPS ORG supplement weight female — indicator gender wbhao — race/ethnicity (white, Black, Hispanic, Asian, ) union — union membership status Steps typical analysis: Download extracts download_cps() Load data load_org() load_cps() Filter relevant sample Calculate weighted statistics using survey weights

title: Template preview
text: ORG (Outgoing Rotation Group) Basic Monthly March (ASEC) May Wage percentiles Employment--population ratios Union wage premiums

title: Template preview
text: Load data. Use load_org() read ORG supplement selected years. Specify variables need keep memory use low. Filter sample. Drop observations zero missing weights. Restrict wage earners estimating wage distribution. Calculate estimates. Use weighted.mean() Hmisc::wtd.quantile() appropriate survey weight. Group year, demographics, characteristics needed.

title: Template preview
text: Load selection years variables CPS ORG extracts: Calculate median wages year: Calculate employment--population ratios race/ethnicity using Basic Monthly data:
code: library(epiextractr) library(dplyr)  cps_org <- load_org(2019:2024, year, month, orgwgt, female, wbhao, wage) cps_org |>   filter(orgwgt > 0, !is.na(wage)) |>   summarize(     median_wage = Hmisc::wtd.quantile(wage, orgwgt, probs = 0.5),     .by = year   ) load_cps("basic", 2010:2024, year, basicwgt, wbhao, emp) |>   filter(basicwgt > 0) |>   summarize(     epop = weighted.mean(emp, w = basicwgt),     .by = c(year, wbhao)   )

title: Template preview
text: Use load_org_sample() small demonstration dataset require downloading full extracts. See epiextractr documentation details.

title: pkgdown Template for EPI R Packages
text: EPI color palette: dark blue navbar, light blue links, neutral grays Figtree font body headings (bundled, CDN dependency) Fira Code font code blocks (bundled) Flat design minimal shadows Light footer standard pkgdown attribution

title: pkgdown Template for EPI R Packages
code: # install from r-universe install.packages("epitemplate", repos = "https://economic.r-universe.dev")  # or install from GitHub pak::pak("economic/epitemplate")

epitemplate 0.0.0.9000
text: Initial template EPI branding: color palette, Figtree typography, Fira Code code blocks, dark navbar, flat design, light footer. Added test() function visual theme testing reference page. Added template preview vignette showing text, bullets, code examples.
